heading: Explanation of medicare benefits
label: 1395b–7 Explanation of medicare benefits

L. 108–173, title IX, § 925117 Stat. 2396Dec. 8, 2003“(a).—In GeneralThe Secretary [of Health and Human Services] shall provide that in medicare beneficiary notices provided (under section 1806(a) of the Social Security Act, ) with respect to the provision of post-hospital extended care services under part A of title XVIII of the Social Security Act [ et seq.], there shall be included information on the number of days of coverage of such services remaining under such part for the medicare beneficiary and spell of illness involved.42 U.S.C. 1395b–7(a)42 U.S.C. 1395c“(b).—Effective DateSubsection (a) shall apply to notices provided during calendar quarters beginning more than 6 months after the date of the enactment of this Act [].”Dec. 8, 2003

children.text: lists the item or service for which payment has been made and the amount of such payment for each item or service; and
includes a notice of the individual’s right to request an itemized statement (as provided in subsection (b)).

children.heading: Request for itemized statement for medicare items and services
children.label: b Request for itemized statement for medicare items and services

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: An individual may submit a written request to any physician, provider, supplier, or any other person (including an organization, agency, or other entity) for an itemized statement for any item or service provided to such individual by such person with respect to which payment has been made under this subchapter.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Not later than 30 days after the date on which a request under paragraph (1) has been made, a person described in such paragraph shall furnish an itemized statement describing each item or service provided to the individual requesting the itemized statement.

children.heading: Penalty
children.label: B Penalty
children.text: Whoever knowingly fails to furnish an itemized statement in accordance with subparagraph (A) shall be subject to a civil money penalty of not more than $100 for each such failure. Such penalty shall be imposed and collected in the same manner as civil money penalties under subsection (a) of are imposed and collected under that section.section 1320a–7a of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Not later than 90 days after the receipt of an itemized statement furnished under paragraph (1), an individual may submit a written request for a review of the itemized statement to the Secretary.

children.label: i
children.text: specific items or services that the individual believes were not provided as claimed, or

children.label: ii
children.text: any other billing irregularity (including duplicate billing).

children.heading: Findings of Secretary
children.label: 4 Findings of Secretary
children.text: The Secretary shall, with respect to each written request submitted under paragraph (3), determine whether the itemized statement identifies specific items or services that were not provided as claimed or any other billing irregularity (including duplicate billing) that has resulted in unnecessary payments under this subchapter.

children.heading: Recovery of amounts
children.label: 5 Recovery of amounts
children.text: The Secretary shall take all appropriate measures to recover amounts unnecessarily paid under this subchapter with respect to a statement described in paragraph (4).

children.heading: Format of statements from Secretary
children.label: c Format of statements from Secretary

children.heading: Electronic option beginning in 2016
children.label: 1 Electronic option beginning in 2016
children.text: Subject to paragraph (2), for statements described in subsection (a) that are furnished for a period in 2016 or a subsequent year, in the case that an individual described in subsection (a) elects, in accordance with such form, manner, and time specified by the Secretary, to receive such statement in an electronic format, such statement shall be furnished to such individual for each period subsequent to such election in such a format and shall not be mailed to the individual.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (B), the Secretary may determine a maximum number of elections described in paragraph (1) by an individual that may be revoked by the individual.

children.heading: Minimum of one revocation option
children.label: B Minimum of one revocation option
children.text: In no case may the Secretary determine a maximum number under subparagraph (A) that is less than one.

children.heading: Notification
children.label: 3 Notification
children.text: The Secretary shall ensure that, in the most cost effective manner and beginning , a clear notification of the option to elect to receive statements described in subsection (a) in an electronic format is made available, such as through the notices distributed under , to individuals described in subsection (a).January 1, 2017section 1395b–2 of this title
